task_completion.visual_evidence: Browser window displaying the OpenCAD login page at http://localhost.
OpenCAD dashboard or authenticated area visible, indicating successful login as admin.
The Data Manager section of OpenCAD is displayed on the screen.

task_completion.description: Access the Streets section within the Data Manager
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Streets management page or tab is active and visible within the Data Manager.

task_completion.description: Open the interface to add a new street
task_completion.visual_evidence: A form, modal, or input field specifically for adding a new street is visible.

task_completion.description: Verify the new street was successfully added
task_completion.visual_evidence: The street name 'Quarry Ridge Haul Rd' is visible in the list of streets within the Data Manager.

integrity.description: Agent used the web GUI to add the street
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the web browser and OpenCAD interface, not terminal commands like curl or mysql.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual OpenCAD application
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser address bar shows http://localhost and the standard OpenCAD UI is present, rather than a local text editor or mock HTML file.

integrity.description: Agent did not use a direct database client to bypass the UI
integrity.visual_evidence: No database management tools (like phpMyAdmin, DBeaver, or sqlite3 CLI) are used to insert the street record directly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected street name is 'Quarry Ridge Haul Rd'. This street does not exist in the default GTAV dataset imported by OpenCAD, so the agent must manually create it using the 'Add Street' functionality rather than searching for it.
